company.name: MediVault Health
company.description: MediVault is an AI-powered clinical decision support platform that integrates with hospital EHR systems to flag misdiagnoses, drug interactions, and billing anomalies in real-time. Our proprietary diagnostic engine has analyzed 14M+ patient encounters and claims 94% accuracy in catching preventable adverse events.

pitch_claims.market_size_claim: $45B global clinical decision support market by 2030
pitch_claims.unique_selling_point: AI diagnostic engine with 94% adverse-event detection rate — 3x better than legacy CDS tools. Seamless EHR integration in under 72 hours.

customer_revenue_breakdown.note: Master agreement with 90-day termination-for-convenience clause. HCA CFO flagged 'vendor consolidation initiative' in Q1 2026 earnings call.

customer_revenue_breakdown.contract_type: Pilot — converting to full contract pending board approval in Q3 2026
customer_revenue_breakdown.note: Pilot has been extended twice. Board approval delayed due to competing bid from Epic's built-in CDS module.

customer_revenue_breakdown.customer: Regional hospital systems (14 hospitals, 6 health networks)
customer_revenue_breakdown.note: Average deal size $68k ARR. High churn — 4 of 14 hospitals have issued non-renewal notices for 2027.
Self-serve tier. $199/mo average. 11% monthly churn rate.

unit_economics.note: Enterprise CAC of $47k is healthy, but 14-month payback is long for a company with 6 months of runway. Self-serve clinic tier has CAC of $890 but LTV of only $1,800 (23-month lifespan at $199/mo with 11% churn).

previous_funding.note: Bridge suggests Series B timing pressure. 20% discount signals investor concern about valuation.

red_flags: 64% revenue concentration in single client (HCA) with 90-day termination-for-convenience clause
HCA publicly signaled 'vendor consolidation' on Q1 2026 earnings call — MediVault may not survive the cut
Only 6 months runway at $920k/mo burn — critical cash pressure during fundraise
Bridge note at 20% discount 9 months ago suggests existing investors had valuation concerns
Self-serve clinic tier has 11% monthly churn — unit economics don't work at scale
4 of 14 regional hospitals issued non-renewal notices — net revenue retention declining
14-month payback period is dangerous with only 6 months of cash

legal.ip_status: 3 utility patents granted (US11,891,234; US11,902,567; US11,945,890) covering diagnostic inference pipeline. 2 additional provisional patents pending. However, core training dataset sourced from licensed hospital data under agreements that restrict commercial sublicensing — potential IP contamination if license terms are challenged.

pending_litigation.case: Doe v. MediVault Health Inc (Case No. 1:2025cv08934, S.D. Texas)
pending_litigation.claim: Family of deceased patient alleges MediVault's AI flagged a drug interaction warning but the alert was dismissed by attending physician. Plaintiff claims the alert UI was 'misleading and easily confused with routine notifications' contributing to patient death.
pending_litigation.status: Pre-trial motions — defendant's motion to dismiss denied Feb 2026. Discovery ongoing.
pending_litigation.note: First 'AI-assisted malpractice' wrongful death case in Texas. If plaintiff prevails, precedent could expose MediVault to product liability for every AI recommendation. Insurance coverage unclear.

pending_litigation.case: United States ex rel. Ramirez v. MediVault Health Inc (Case No. 4:2026cv02103, N.D. Texas)
pending_litigation.claim: Former employee (billing analyst) alleges MediVault knowingly inflated 'billing anomaly detection' accuracy metrics in marketing materials presented to Medicare-participating hospitals, causing those hospitals to overbill Medicare based on MediVault's recommendations.
pending_litigation.status: Under seal — DOJ reviewing whether to intervene. If DOJ intervenes, liability triples under False Claims Act.
pending_litigation.note: False Claims Act provides treble damages + $11k-$23k per false claim. Whistleblower was terminated in Jan 2026 — potential retaliation claim adds exposure. This lawsuit was NOT disclosed in the pitch materials.

regulatory_compliance.hipaa: Self-assessed as compliant. No independent HIPAA audit conducted. Last internal review: March 2025 — 14 months ago.
regulatory_compliance.fda_status: MediVault positions its product as 'clinical decision support' exempt from FDA 510(k) clearance under 21st Century Cures Act criteria. However, the diagnostic engine's autonomous flagging of misdiagnoses may cross the line into 'software as a medical device' (SaMD) requiring FDA clearance. FDA has been increasingly aggressive about reclassifying AI diagnostic tools in 2025-2026.
regulatory_compliance.state_regulations: Telehealth-adjacent regulations in 6 states (NY, CA, TX, FL, IL, MA) may require additional licensing for AI-driven clinical recommendations.
regulatory_compliance.cms_requirements: CMS issued draft rule in March 2026 requiring AI clinical tools used in Medicare-participating facilities to undergo algorithmic bias testing. MediVault has not conducted bias audits.

data_privacy.soc2: Type I certified (point-in-time, June 2024). Type II audit (continuous) never completed — auditor flagged 'material control deficiencies' and MediVault chose not to proceed.
data_privacy.hipaa_baa: BAAs in place with all hospital clients, but 3 subprocessor agreements (AWS, Anthropic API, and MongoDB Atlas) have terms that may conflict with BAA obligations regarding PHI processing.
data_privacy.data_residency: Patient data processed in us-east-1 (Virginia) and us-west-2 (Oregon). No data residency controls for state-specific healthcare privacy laws.

cap_table.note: Founders at 38% pre-Series B — will dilute to ~28% post-raise. Existing a16z board seat has standard protective provisions + 1x non-participating liquidation preference. Bridge note converts at 20% discount to this round.

red_flags: Wrongful death lawsuit ($15M) — first AI-malpractice case in Texas, precedent-setting risk
UNDISCLOSED False Claims Act whistleblower suit ($30M treble damages) — DOJ reviewing intervention
Whistleblower was terminated — retaliation claim adds exposure
FDA reclassification risk — product may require 510(k) clearance as 'software as medical device'
SOC2 Type II audit abandoned after auditor found 'material control deficiencies'
HIPAA compliance is self-assessed only — no independent audit in 14 months
Anthropic API subprocessor agreement may conflict with HIPAA BAA for PHI processing
CMS algorithmic bias testing requirement not addressed

tech_stack.backend: Python 3.9 (end-of-life October 2025 — running on unsupported runtime)
tech_stack.database: MongoDB Atlas M50 cluster (production) + PostgreSQL 14 for billing
tech_stack.infrastructure: AWS EKS (Kubernetes) — 3 clusters, us-east-1 and us-west-2
tech_stack.llm_integration: Anthropic Claude 3 Haiku for natural language summaries of diagnostic findings — PHI passes through Anthropic API
tech_stack.ehr_integration: HL7 FHIR R4 connectors for Epic, Cerner, Meditech. Custom HL7v2 ADT adapters for legacy systems.

security.last_penetration_test: August 2024 — Rapid7 found 3 critical, 8 high-severity vulnerabilities. As of June 2026, 2 of 3 critical vulnerabilities remain unpatched.
security.pii_storage: PHI encrypted at rest (AES-256) in MongoDB Atlas. However, diagnostic inference logs contain patient identifiers in plaintext and are retained indefinitely in CloudWatch Logs — accessible to all engineers with AWS console access.
security.encryption_in_transit: TLS 1.2 enforced for API endpoints. Internal service-to-service communication uses mTLS. However, HL7v2 legacy adapters for 4 hospital clients transmit data over unencrypted TCP connections (MLLP without TLS wrapping).
security.mfa: Enforced for production AWS access. NOT enforced for MongoDB Atlas admin, internal Slack, or GitHub — all of which contain PHI references in logs/discussions.
security.soc2: Type I only (see legal section). Type II abandoned.
security.hitrust: Not certified. 2 hospital prospects (Mayo Clinic, Cleveland Clinic) declined to proceed without HITRUST CSF certification.
security.incident_history: March 2025: Engineer accidentally pushed production MongoDB connection string (including credentials) to public GitHub repository. Credentials were valid for 9 hours before rotation. No evidence of unauthorized access, but incident was not reported to affected hospital clients as required by BAA terms.
security.ai_model_security: Diagnostic model weights stored in S3 with default bucket policies. No model versioning or integrity verification. Adversarial input testing never conducted.

architecture.pattern: Microservices (12 services in Kubernetes). Diagnostic inference service is a monolithic Python app processing ~2,100 requests/minute at peak.
architecture.scalability: Inference service auto-scales to 8 pods but latency degrades beyond 1,800 req/min (p99 jumps from 340ms to 2.4s). HCA contract requires <500ms p99 — currently in breach during peak hours.
architecture.single_points_of_failure: Central FHIR gateway — if it goes down, all 42 HCA hospitals lose diagnostic support simultaneously. No circuit breaker or failover implemented.
architecture.tech_debt_assessment: MEDIUM-HIGH — original CTO (co-founder) departed in Jan 2025 and was replaced by VP Eng promoted internally. Significant undocumented 'tribal knowledge' in inference pipeline. 34% of codebase has no test coverage.
architecture.uptime_sla: 99.9% SLA in HCA contract. Actual uptime last 12 months: 99.4%. 3 incidents exceeded 4-hour resolution target. Penalty clauses have not been enforced yet but HCA has logged formal service credits totaling $180k.

red_flags: Python 3.9 EOL — running production healthcare system on unsupported runtime
2 of 3 critical pentest vulnerabilities unpatched for 22 months
PHI in plaintext CloudWatch Logs accessible to all engineers — HIPAA violation
4 hospital HL7v2 connections transmit PHI over unencrypted TCP — HIPAA violation
MongoDB credentials leaked to public GitHub — not disclosed to hospital clients per BAA
PHI sent through Anthropic Claude API — potential HIPAA BAA conflict
Breaching HCA's <500ms SLA during peak hours — $180k in service credits already logged
Original CTO departed — inference pipeline has significant tribal knowledge risk
No adversarial testing on AI model that makes clinical recommendations

actual_market_trends.ai_healthcare_funding_2025: Down 31% YoY as investors rotate to AI infrastructure plays
actual_market_trends.hospital_it_budgets: Flat to declining in 2025-2026 — hospitals prioritizing cost reduction post-COVID margin compression
actual_market_trends.ehr_vendor_competition: Epic (62% market share) launched built-in AI diagnostic module 'Cognitive Computing' in Sept 2025 — included at no additional cost for existing customers. Cerner/Oracle Health following in Q3 2026.
actual_market_trends.regulatory_tailwinds: CMS reimbursement for AI-assisted diagnostics proposed in 2027 rulemaking — if approved, could expand addressable market significantly

competitors.name: Epic Cognitive Computing
competitors.description: Built-in CDS module bundled free with Epic EHR
competitors.threat_level: CRITICAL — Epic owns 62% of hospital EHR market and is bundling AI diagnostics at zero marginal cost
competitors.note: 3 of MediVault's pipeline prospects switched to Epic's free module in Q1 2026

competitors.name: Viz.ai
competitors.status: FDA-cleared for stroke/PE detection. Expanding into general CDS.

competitors.name: Aidoc
competitors.status: FDA-cleared, 1,000+ hospitals. Full AI diagnostic suite.
competitors.note: Direct competitor with FDA clearance advantage

competitors.name: Google Health / DeepMind
competitors.description: Med-PaLM 2 clinical reasoning model available via Google Cloud
competitors.threat_level: HIGH — if Google offers CDS-as-a-service through existing cloud hospital contracts

competitors.name: Nuance DAX (Microsoft)
competitors.description: AI clinical documentation expanding into diagnostic support
competitors.note: Microsoft/Nuance already embedded in hospital workflows — adjacent expansion into CDS likely

regulatory_headwinds.fda_samd: FDA increasingly reclassifying AI clinical tools as 'Software as Medical Device' — MediVault's exemption claim is legally untested
regulatory_headwinds.cms_bias_rule: Draft CMS rule (March 2026) requires algorithmic bias testing for AI tools in Medicare facilities — compliance cost estimated $200k-$800k
regulatory_headwinds.state_ai_laws: California SB-1047 (AI safety) and New York AI accountability laws may impose additional requirements on clinical AI systems in 2026-2027
regulatory_headwinds.eu_ai_act: EU AI Act classifies medical AI as 'high risk' — blocks international expansion without full conformity assessment (estimated 12-18 months, $500k+)

red_flags: Epic bundling free AI diagnostics to 62% of hospital EHR market — existential competitive threat
3 pipeline prospects already switched to Epic's free module in Q1 2026
Direct competitors Viz.ai ($1.2B) and Aidoc ($1.4B) both have FDA clearance — MediVault does not
Hospital IT budgets flat/declining — selling a $200k+ add-on in a cost-cutting environment
AI healthcare VC funding down 31% YoY — fundraising environment unfavorable
TAM claim of $45B by 2030 is 21x the current $2.1B market — implausible without extraordinary tailwinds
Google Health (Med-PaLM) and Microsoft/Nuance are adjacent threats with infinite distribution

ceo.name: Dr. Priya Sharma
ceo.previous_company: McKinsey Healthcare Practice (Principal, 2015-2020), then Chief Medical Officer at Tempus AI (2020-2021)
ceo.assessment: Strong clinical + business pedigree. MD + MBA + McKinsey + Tempus is a credible founding profile. However, first-time CEO — no prior experience scaling a company past $10M ARR or managing 60+ person org.

cto.name: James Wu
cto.previous: Google Brain (Research Scientist, 3 years), Apple Health (Senior ML Engineer, 2 years)
cto.status: DEPARTED — Left MediVault in January 2025 to join Anthropic. Listed as 'Technical Advisor' on website. Actually holds 0 advisory hours/month.
cto.assessment: Strong technical background but NO LONGER WITH THE COMPANY. Departure was not disclosed in pitch deck — he is still listed as CTO on the company website and investor materials.

vp_engineering.name: Rachel Torres
vp_engineering.previous: MediVault (Senior Engineer → VP Eng), previously at Athenahealth (3 years)
vp_engineering.current_role: Promoted to VP Eng after CTO departure. De facto technical leader.
vp_engineering.assessment: Capable engineer but first VP-level role. Managing 22 engineers with 5 years total experience. No ML/AI research background — relies on CTO's original model architecture.

cfo.name: Michael Okonkwo
cfo.previous: Finance Director at Veeva Systems (Series D through IPO), then CFO at CarePort Health (acquired by WellSky for $1.35B)
cfo.assessment: STRONG — one of the best hires. IPO-track experience at Veeva + successful exit at CarePort. Joined MediVault 8 months ago — may have been brought in to prep for Series B.

team_gaps: CTO departed but still listed as active team member in pitch materials — MISREPRESENTATION
No Chief Medical Officer replacing Dr. Sharma's clinical oversight after she shifted to full-time CEO duties
No VP Sales — enterprise sales motion run by CEO directly. Pipeline management is ad hoc.
No Head of Regulatory Affairs — critical gap given FDA/CMS regulatory exposure
No CISO or dedicated security lead — security decisions made by VP Eng who has no security specialization
ML team is 3 engineers maintaining CTO's original model — no senior ML researcher to iterate on core product

deal_summary.existing_investor_participation: a16z Bio Fund indicating $5M follow-on. General Catalyst passing on this round.
deal_summary.additional_context: General Catalyst declining to participate in Series B is a negative signal — they led the Series A at $8.5M. Bridge note at 20% discount 9 months ago further suggests valuation pressure. Company needs to close this round within 6 months or will need another bridge or face down-round risk.
